page: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes AMD EPYC Milan processors running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, meaning physical cores are shared with other tenants. The server includes 800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong memory subsystem and highly capable single-core execution. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the top 10% tier with a score of 3132.97. Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks are also strong, reaching 228.44 GB/sec for read operations and 156.49 GB/sec for write operations, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance and database operations align with average mid-tier expectations, as demonstrated by a multi-core stress-ng score of 25046.38 and a PassMark CPU score of 20591.17.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high density of memory and local NVMe storage relative to its shared CPU allocation. The shared CPU model reduces overall cost, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated, sustained CPU performance. This hardware profile is suitable for database hosting, caching layers such as Redis, static web serving, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.0 GHz, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. It comes equipped with 800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong large-block memory bandwidth, while multi-core metrics and database operations fall within the average tier. This combination of high memory density and local NVMe storage, balanced by a shared CPU model, provides a cost-efficient platform for memory-heavy workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and web serving applications that benefit from high memory capacity without requiring dedicated CPU cores.
